concerns.text: T014 (Data Persistence) is marked [X] (complete) but T027 (Simulation Loop) is marked [ ] (incomplete). T027 explicitly depends on T014 to persist results to `data/synthetic/` for checkpointing. However, T027 is the *producer* of the `results/simulation_results.csv` artifact which T029 (Aggregation) consumes. The current order places T027 *after* T029 in the visual list (Phase 5), but T029 is marked [X] while T027 is [ ]. This implies the consumer (T029) is marked complete before the producer (T027) is implemented, violating the 'Producer before consumer' dependency rule. T027 must precede T029.

T029 (Calculate Aggregate Metrics) is marked [X] (complete) but depends on T027 (Simulation Loop) which is marked [ ] (incomplete). T029 consumes `results/simulation_results.csv` generated by T027. A task cannot be marked complete if its primary input artifact (generated by a pending task) does not exist. T027 must be moved before T029 and marked as a prerequisite.

T031b (Mixed-effects model for real-world data) is marked [ ] (incomplete) but depends on T038 (Reuse pipeline on real data) which is marked [X]. However, T038 depends on T034b (Orchestrate Real-World Ingestion) which is marked [ ]. T031b requires `results/real_world_results.csv` which is generated by T038. The dependency chain T034b -> T038 -> T031b is broken because T034b is incomplete. T034b must be completed before T038 and T031b can proceed.

T034a (Create Dataset Configuration) is marked [X] but T034b (Orchestrate Ingestion) is marked [ ]. T034b explicitly depends on T034a. While T034a is complete, T034b is the consumer of that config. The issue is that T034b is marked incomplete, blocking T038 and T031b. This is a valid dependency chain, but the status of T034b being incomplete while downstream tasks (T038) are marked complete (or partially complete) creates a logical inconsistency in the 'complete' state of the phase. T034b must be addressed before T038 is considered fully executable.

T027 is marked [ ] (incomplete) but is listed *after* T029 (marked [X]) in the task list. While the 'Dependencies' section at the bottom correctly notes T027 depends on US1/US2, the visual ordering within Phase 5 places the consumer (T029) before the producer (T027). Reorder tasks within Phase 5 so T027 (Orchestration) appears before T029 (Aggregation) to reflect the data flow.

T034b is marked [ ] (incomplete) but is listed *after* T038 (marked [X]) in the task list. T038 depends on T034b (ingestion must happen before testing). The visual order implies T038 can run before T034b. Reorder to place T034b before T038.
